content.claim: Hungary tracks students earlier than almost any OECD comparator, with partial tracking at Grade 5 (age 10-11) or Grade 7 (age 12-13), compared to the OECD average of age 15, and approximately 85% of Hungarian students attend schools with academic selection criteria versus 43% OECD average.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Finnish system reform lens
limitations: Percentages cited in research notes lack direct source attribution in original materials
Data points may reflect different measurement years across sources
Comparison methodology across countries not specified in notes
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
